{{Otheruses|Naka-Hikawa Shrine in Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa|Naka-Hikawa Shrine in Mikajima, Tokorozawa|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)}} {{神社 |名称 = Naka-Hikawa Shrine |画像 = [[File:Naka-Hikawa-jinja (Tokorozawa Yamaguchi) 01.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Tokorozawa|Q165857}}Yamaguchi 1849 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small) candidate shrine
former {{jalink|kensha}} |創建 = traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{jalink|taisha-zukuri|Q387874}} |別名 = |例祭 = First Sunday of April, and Sports Day in October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=46|lat_sec=43.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.8|label=Naka-Hikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)}} '''Naka-Hikawa Shrine''' (なかひかわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} of {{jalink|Hikawa faith|Q11549596}} in {{jalink|Yamaguchi (Tokorozawa)|Q11466558|Yamaguchi}}, {{jalink|Tokorozawa|Q165857}}, {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}. It was the general guardian of 92 villages in former {{jalink|Iruma District|Q1153801}} and {{jalink|Tama District|Q11430789}}, and was a former {{jalink|kensha}}. === Overview === * It is a candidate shrine for Naka-Hikawa-sha, a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. Like Ōmiya {{jalink|Hikawa Shrine|Q703633}}, it enshrines three deities: {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Kushinadahime|Q505469|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}}. * "Naka" in Naka-Hikawa is explained as: midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and {{jalink|Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942}} ({{jalink|Okutama|Q598626}}, {{jalink|Nishitama District|Q1048689}}, {{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}}), or midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and the {{jalink|provincial capital|Q1629088}} of {{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}} ({{jalink|Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|Fuchū}}). * Locally it has long been called "Hikawa-sama." === History === * Traditionally founded in the reign of Emperor Sujin. * In late {{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}}, {{jalink|Yamaguchi Ietsugu|Q11466765}} built shrine buildings. They later burned in warfare, but in the {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} era (1573-1593), {{jalink|Yamaguchi Takatada|Q11467543}} restored it together with {{jalink|Yamaguchi Castle (Musashi)|Q11466729|Yamaguchi Castle}}. * In {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 20 ({{jalink|1945|Q5240}}), Brigadier General Ken R. Dyke, first director of GHQ/CIE, inspected this shrine's festival. ===Shrine buildings / precinct=== *Honden: {{jalink|taisha-zukuri|Q387874}}, built in 1927. *Shichisha Shrine: moved into the precincts from an area scheduled to be submerged by {{jalink|Yamaguchi Reservoir|Q11467481}}. ===Festivals=== *Spring grand festival is on the first Sunday of April; autumn grand festival is on Sports Day in October. ===Location / transportation=== *Saitama Prefecture, Tokorozawa, Yamaguchi 1849 **15 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Shimo-Yamaguchi Station|Q7496941}} on the {{jalink|Seibu Sayama Line|Q1965549}} ===Managed / served shrines=== *Managed shrines **{{jalink|Sengen Shrine (Tokorozawa)||Sengen Shrine}} (Arahata Fuji) **{{jalink|Tenma Tenjin Shrine (Tokorozawa)||Tenma Tenjin Shrine}} *Served shrine (outside precinct) **{{jalink|Atago Gongen-sha (Tokorozawa)|Q17191381|Atago Gongen-sha}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{jalink|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943}}|volume=Vol. 158, Iruma District No.3|chapter=Hikawa village, Hikawa shrine|id={{NDLJP|764001/72}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} ===External links=== *[http://nakahikawa.or.jp/ Naka-Hikawa Shrine homepage] ===See also=== *{{jalink|Hikawa faith|Q11549596}} *{{jalink|Hikawa Shrine|Q703633}} *{{jalink|Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942}} *{{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334}} *{{jalink|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)|Q17226968}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかひかわしんしや ところさわし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の氷川神社]] [[Category:所沢市の歴史]] [[Category:所沢市の建築物]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{Otheruses|Naka-Hikawa Shrine in Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa|Naka-Hikawa Shrine in Mikajima, Tokorozawa|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)}} {{神社 |名称 = Naka-Hikawa Shrine |画像 = Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Tokorozawa|Q165857}}Yamaguchi 1849 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small) candidate shrine
former {{jalink|kensha}} |創建 = traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{jalink|taisha-zukuri|Q387874}} |別名 = |例祭 = First Sunday of April, and Sports Day in October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=46|lat_sec=43.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.8|label=Naka-Hikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)}} '''Naka-Hikawa Shrine''' (なかひかわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} of {{jalink|Hikawa faith|Q11549596}} in {{jalink|Yamaguchi (Tokorozawa)|Q11466558|Yamaguchi}}, {{jalink|Tokorozawa|Q165857}}, {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}. It was the general guardian of 92 villages in former {{jalink|Iruma District|Q1153801}} and {{jalink|Tama District|Q11430789}}, and was a former {{jalink|kensha}}. === Overview === * It is a candidate shrine for Naka-Hikawa-sha, a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. Like Ōmiya {{jalink|Hikawa Shrine|Q703633}}, it enshrines three deities: {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Kushinadahime|Q505469|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}}. * "Naka" in Naka-Hikawa is explained as: midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and {{jalink|Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942}} ({{jalink|Okutama|Q598626}}, {{jalink|Nishitama District|Q1048689}}, {{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}}), or midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and the {{jalink|provincial capital|Q1629088}} of {{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}} ({{jalink|Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|Fuchū}}). * Locally it has long been called "Hikawa-sama." === History === * Traditionally founded in the reign of Emperor Sujin. * In late {{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}}, {{jalink|Yamaguchi Ietsugu|Q11466765}} built shrine buildings. They later burned in warfare, but in the {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} era (1573-1593), {{jalink|Yamaguchi Takatada|Q11467543}} restored it together with {{jalink|Yamaguchi Castle (Musashi)|Q11466729|Yamaguchi Castle}}. * In {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 20 ({{jalink|1945|Q5240}}), Brigadier General Ken R. Dyke, first director of GHQ/CIE, inspected this shrine's festival. ===Shrine buildings / precinct=== *Honden: {{jalink|taisha-zukuri|Q387874}}, built in 1927. *Shichisha Shrine: moved into the precincts from an area scheduled to be submerged by {{jalink|Yamaguchi Reservoir|Q11467481}}. ===Festivals=== *Spring grand festival is on the first Sunday of April; autumn grand festival is on Sports Day in October. ===Location / transportation=== *Saitama Prefecture, Tokorozawa, Yamaguchi 1849 **15 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Shimo-Yamaguchi Station|Q7496941}} on the {{jalink|Seibu Sayama Line|Q1965549}} ===Managed / served shrines=== *Managed shrines **{{jalink|Sengen Shrine (Tokorozawa)||Sengen Shrine}} (Arahata Fuji) **{{jalink|Tenma Tenjin Shrine (Tokorozawa)||Tenma Tenjin Shrine}} *Served shrine (outside precinct) **{{jalink|Atago Gongen-sha (Tokorozawa)|Q17191381|Atago Gongen-sha}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{jalink|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943}}|volume=Vol. 158, Iruma District No.3|chapter=Hikawa village, Hikawa shrine|id={{NDLJP|764001/72}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} ===External links=== *[http://nakahikawa.or.jp/ Naka-Hikawa Shrine homepage] ===See also=== *{{jalink|Hikawa faith|Q11549596}} *{{jalink|Hikawa Shrine|Q703633}} *{{jalink|Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942}} *{{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334}} *{{jalink|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)|Q17226968}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかひかわしんしや ところさわし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の氷川神社]] [[Category:所沢市の歴史]] [[Category:所沢市の建築物]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]] [[Category:need translation]]

* {'text': '埼玉県所沢市山口1850', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Naka-hikawa-jinja (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)}} [[JA:中氷川神社 (所沢市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11366320}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|Naka-Hikawa Shrine in Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa|Naka-Hikawa Shrine in Mikajima, Tokorozawa|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)}} {{神社 |名称 = Naka-Hikawa Shrine |画像 = [[File:Naka-Hikawa-jinja (Tokorozawa Yamaguchi) 01.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Tokorozawa|Q165857}}Yamaguchi 1849 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small) candidate shrine
former {{jalink|kensha}} |創建 = traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{jalink|taisha-zukuri|Q387874}} |別名 = |例祭 = First Sunday of April, and Sports Day in October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=46|lat_sec=43.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.8|label=Naka-Hikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)}} '''Naka-Hikawa Shrine''' (なかひかわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} of {{jalink|Hikawa faith|Q11549596}} in {{jalink|Yamaguchi (Tokorozawa)|Q11466558|Yamaguchi}}, {{jalink|Tokorozawa|Q165857}}, {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}. It was the general guardian of 92 villages in former {{jalink|Iruma District|Q1153801}} and {{jalink|Tama District|Q11430789}}, and was a former {{jalink|kensha}}. === Overview === * It is a candidate shrine for Naka-Hikawa-sha, a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. Like Ōmiya {{jalink|Hikawa Shrine|Q703633}}, it enshrines three deities: {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Kushinadahime|Q505469|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}}. * "Naka" in Naka-Hikawa is explained as: midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and {{jalink|Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942}} ({{jalink|Okutama|Q598626}}, {{jalink|Nishitama District|Q1048689}}, {{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}}), or midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and the {{jalink|provincial capital|Q1629088}} of {{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}} ({{jalink|Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|Fuchū}}). * Locally it has long been called "Hikawa-sama." === History === * Traditionally founded in the reign of Emperor Sujin. * In late {{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}}, {{jalink|Yamaguchi Ietsugu|Q11466765}} built shrine buildings. They later burned in warfare, but in the {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} era (1573-1593), {{jalink|Yamaguchi Takatada|Q11467543}} restored it together with {{jalink|Yamaguchi Castle (Musashi)|Q11466729|Yamaguchi Castle}}. * In {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 20 ({{jalink|1945|Q5240}}), Brigadier General Ken R. Dyke, first director of GHQ/CIE, inspected this shrine's festival. ===Shrine buildings / precinct=== *Honden: {{jalink|taisha-zukuri|Q387874}}, built in 1927. *Shichisha Shrine: moved into the precincts from an area scheduled to be submerged by {{jalink|Yamaguchi Reservoir|Q11467481}}. ===Festivals=== *Spring grand festival is on the first Sunday of April; autumn grand festival is on Sports Day in October. ===Location / transportation=== *Saitama Prefecture, Tokorozawa, Yamaguchi 1849 **15 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Shimo-Yamaguchi Station|Q7496941}} on the {{jalink|Seibu Sayama Line|Q1965549}} ===Managed / served shrines=== *Managed shrines **{{jalink|Sengen Shrine (Tokorozawa)||Sengen Shrine}} (Arahata Fuji) **{{jalink|Tenma Tenjin Shrine (Tokorozawa)||Tenma Tenjin Shrine}} *Served shrine (outside precinct) **{{jalink|Atago Gongen-sha (Tokorozawa)|Q17191381|Atago Gongen-sha}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{jalink|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943}}|volume=Vol. 158, Iruma District No.3|chapter=Hikawa village, Hikawa shrine|id={{NDLJP|764001/72}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} ===External links=== *[http://nakahikawa.or.jp/ Naka-Hikawa Shrine homepage] ===See also=== *{{jalink|Hikawa faith|Q11549596}} *{{jalink|Hikawa Shrine|Q703633}} *{{jalink|Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942}} *{{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334}} *{{jalink|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)|Q17226968}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかひかわしんしや ところさわし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の氷川神社]] [[Category:所沢市の歴史]] [[Category:所沢市の建築物]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]]

Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Tokorozawa|Q165857}}Yamaguchi 1849 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small) candidate shrine
former {{jalink|kensha}} |創建 = traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{jalink|taisha-zukuri|Q387874}} |別名 = |例祭 = First Sunday of April, and Sports Day in October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=46|lat_sec=43.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.8|label=Naka-Hikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)}} '''Naka-Hikawa Shrine''' (なかひかわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} of {{jalink|Hikawa faith|Q11549596}} in {{jalink|Yamaguchi (Tokorozawa)|Q11466558|Yamaguchi}}, {{jalink|Tokorozawa|Q165857}}, {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}. It was the general guardian of 92 villages in former {{jalink|Iruma District|Q1153801}} and {{jalink|Tama District|Q11430789}}, and was a former {{jalink|kensha}}. === Overview === * It is a candidate shrine for Naka-Hikawa-sha, a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. Like Ōmiya {{jalink|Hikawa Shrine|Q703633}}, it enshrines three deities: {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Kushinadahime|Q505469|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}}. * "Naka" in Naka-Hikawa is explained as: midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and {{jalink|Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942}} ({{jalink|Okutama|Q598626}}, {{jalink|Nishitama District|Q1048689}}, {{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}}), or midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and the {{jalink|provincial capital|Q1629088}} of {{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}} ({{jalink|Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|Fuchū}}). * Locally it has long been called "Hikawa-sama." === History === * Traditionally founded in the reign of Emperor Sujin. * In late {{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}}, {{jalink|Yamaguchi Ietsugu|Q11466765}} built shrine buildings. They later burned in warfare, but in the {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} era (1573-1593), {{jalink|Yamaguchi Takatada|Q11467543}} restored it together with {{jalink|Yamaguchi Castle (Musashi)|Q11466729|Yamaguchi Castle}}. * In {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 20 ({{jalink|1945|Q5240}}), Brigadier General Ken R. Dyke, first director of GHQ/CIE, inspected this shrine's festival. ===Shrine buildings / precinct=== *Honden: {{jalink|taisha-zukuri|Q387874}}, built in 1927. *Shichisha Shrine: moved into the precincts from an area scheduled to be submerged by {{jalink|Yamaguchi Reservoir|Q11467481}}. ===Festivals=== *Spring grand festival is on the first Sunday of April; autumn grand festival is on Sports Day in October. ===Location / transportation=== *Saitama Prefecture, Tokorozawa, Yamaguchi 1849 **15 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Shimo-Yamaguchi Station|Q7496941}} on the {{jalink|Seibu Sayama Line|Q1965549}} ===Managed / served shrines=== *Managed shrines **{{jalink|Sengen Shrine (Tokorozawa)||Sengen Shrine}} (Arahata Fuji) **{{jalink|Tenma Tenjin Shrine (Tokorozawa)||Tenma Tenjin Shrine}} *Served shrine (outside precinct) **{{jalink|Atago Gongen-sha (Tokorozawa)|Q17191381|Atago Gongen-sha}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{jalink|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943}}|volume=Vol. 158, Iruma District No.3|chapter=Hikawa village, Hikawa shrine|id={{NDLJP|764001/72}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} ===External links=== *[http://nakahikawa.or.jp/ Naka-Hikawa Shrine homepage] ===See also=== *{{jalink|Hikawa faith|Q11549596}} *{{jalink|Hikawa Shrine|Q703633}} *{{jalink|Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942}} *{{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334}} *{{jalink|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)|Q17226968}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかひかわしんしや ところさわし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の氷川神社]] [[Category:所沢市の歴史]] [[Category:所沢市の建築物]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]]

{{Otheruses|Naka-Hikawa Shrine in Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa|Naka-Hikawa Shrine in Mikajima, Tokorozawa|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)}} {{神社 |名称 = Naka-Hikawa Shrine |画像 = Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Tokorozawa|Q165857}}Yamaguchi 1849 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small) candidate shrine
former {{jalink|kensha}} |創建 = traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{jalink|taisha-zukuri|Q387874}} |別名 = |例祭 = First Sunday of April, and Sports Day in October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=46|lat_sec=43.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.8|label=Naka-Hikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)}} '''Naka-Hikawa Shrine''' (なかひかわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} of {{jalink|Hikawa faith|Q11549596}} in {{jalink|Yamaguchi (Tokorozawa)|Q11466558|Yamaguchi}}, {{jalink|Tokorozawa|Q165857}}, {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}. It was the general guardian of 92 villages in former {{jalink|Iruma District|Q1153801}} and {{jalink|Tama District|Q11430789}}, and was a former {{jalink|kensha}}. === Overview === * It is a candidate shrine for Naka-Hikawa-sha, a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. Like Ōmiya {{jalink|Hikawa Shrine|Q703633}}, it enshrines three deities: {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Kushinadahime|Q505469|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}}. * "Naka" in Naka-Hikawa is explained as: midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and {{jalink|Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942}} ({{jalink|Okutama|Q598626}}, {{jalink|Nishitama District|Q1048689}}, {{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}}), or midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and the {{jalink|provincial capital|Q1629088}} of {{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}} ({{jalink|Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|Fuchū}}). * Locally it has long been called "Hikawa-sama." === History === * Traditionally founded in the reign of Emperor Sujin. * In late {{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}}, {{jalink|Yamaguchi Ietsugu|Q11466765}} built shrine buildings. They later burned in warfare, but in the {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} era (1573-1593), {{jalink|Yamaguchi Takatada|Q11467543}} restored it together with {{jalink|Yamaguchi Castle (Musashi)|Q11466729|Yamaguchi Castle}}. * In {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 20 ({{jalink|1945|Q5240}}), Brigadier General Ken R. Dyke, first director of GHQ/CIE, inspected this shrine's festival. ===Shrine buildings / precinct=== *Honden: {{jalink|taisha-zukuri|Q387874}}, built in 1927. *Shichisha Shrine: moved into the precincts from an area scheduled to be submerged by {{jalink|Yamaguchi Reservoir|Q11467481}}. ===Festivals=== *Spring grand festival is on the first Sunday of April; autumn grand festival is on Sports Day in October. ===Location / transportation=== *Saitama Prefecture, Tokorozawa, Yamaguchi 1849 **15 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Shimo-Yamaguchi Station|Q7496941}} on the {{jalink|Seibu Sayama Line|Q1965549}} ===Managed / served shrines=== *Managed shrines **{{jalink|Sengen Shrine (Tokorozawa)||Sengen Shrine}} (Arahata Fuji) **{{jalink|Tenma Tenjin Shrine (Tokorozawa)||Tenma Tenjin Shrine}} *Served shrine (outside precinct) **{{jalink|Atago Gongen-sha (Tokorozawa)|Q17191381|Atago Gongen-sha}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{jalink|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943}}|volume=Vol. 158, Iruma District No.3|chapter=Hikawa village, Hikawa shrine|id={{NDLJP|764001/72}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} ===External links=== *[http://nakahikawa.or.jp/ Naka-Hikawa Shrine homepage] ===See also=== *{{jalink|Hikawa faith|Q11549596}} *{{jalink|Hikawa Shrine|Q703633}} *{{jalink|Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942}} *{{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334}} *{{jalink|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)|Q17226968}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかひかわしんしや ところさわし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の氷川神社]] [[Category:所沢市の歴史]] [[Category:所沢市の建築物]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857}}Yamaguchi 1849 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill||lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}
{{ill||lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}
{{ill||lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small) candidate shrine
former {{ill||lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}} |創建 = traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874}} |別名 = |例祭 = First Sunday of April, and Sports Day in October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=46|lat_sec=43.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.8|label=Naka-Hikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)}} '''Naka-Hikawa Shrine''' (なかひかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} of {{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596}} in {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi (Tokorozawa)|Q11466558|WD=Q11466558|lt=Yamaguchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}. It was the general guardian of 92 villages in former {{ill|lt=Iruma District|Q1153801|WD=Q1153801}} and {{ill|lt=Tama District|Q11430789|WD=Q11430789}}, and was a former {{ill||lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}}. === Overview === * It is a candidate shrine for Naka-Hikawa-sha, a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}. Like Ōmiya {{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633}}, it enshrines three deities: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}}. * "Naka" in Naka-Hikawa is explained as: midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and {{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942}} ({{ill|lt=Okutama|Q598626|WD=Q598626}}, {{ill|lt=Nishitama District|Q1048689|WD=Q1048689}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}), or midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and the {{ill|lt=provincial capital|Q1629088|WD=Q1629088}} of {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}} ({{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū}}). * Locally it has long been called "Hikawa-sama." === History === * Traditionally founded in the reign of Emperor Sujin. * In late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Ietsugu|Q11466765|WD=Q11466765}} built shrine buildings. They later burned in warfare, but in the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} era (1573-1593), {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Takatada|Q11467543|WD=Q11467543}} restored it together with {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Castle (Musashi)|Q11466729|WD=Q11466729|lt=Yamaguchi Castle}}. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 20 ({{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240}}), Brigadier General Ken R. Dyke, first director of GHQ/CIE, inspected this shrine's festival. ===Shrine buildings / precinct=== *Honden: {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874}}, built in 1927. *Shichisha Shrine: moved into the precincts from an area scheduled to be submerged by {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Reservoir|Q11467481|WD=Q11467481}}. ===Festivals=== *Spring grand festival is on the first Sunday of April; autumn grand festival is on Sports Day in October. ===Location / transportation=== *Saitama Prefecture, Tokorozawa, Yamaguchi 1849 **15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shimo-Yamaguchi Station|Q7496941|WD=Q7496941}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Sayama Line|Q1965549|WD=Q1965549}} ===Managed / served shrines=== *Managed shrines **{{ill|lt=Sengen Shrine (Tokorozawa)||WD=|lt=Sengen Shrine}} (Arahata Fuji) **{{ill|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine (Tokorozawa)||WD=|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine}} *Served shrine (outside precinct) **{{ill|lt=Atago Gongen-sha (Tokorozawa)|Q17191381|WD=Q17191381|lt=Atago Gongen-sha}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943}}|volume=Vol. 158, Iruma District No.3|chapter=Hikawa village, Hikawa shrine|id={{NDLJP|764001/72}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} ===External links=== *[http://nakahikawa.or.jp/ Naka-Hikawa Shrine homepage] ===See also=== *{{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596}} *{{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633}} *{{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942}} *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334}} *{{ill|lt=Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)|Q17226968|WD=Q17226968}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかひかわしんしや ところさわし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の氷川神社]] [[Category:所沢市の歴史]] [[Category:所沢市の建築物]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{Otheruses|Naka-Hikawa Shrine in Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa|Naka-Hikawa Shrine in Mikajima, Tokorozawa|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)}} {{神社 |名称 = Naka-Hikawa Shrine |画像 = Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857}}Yamaguchi 1849 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small) candidate shrine
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}} |創建 = traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874}} |別名 = |例祭 = First Sunday of April, and Sports Day in October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=46|lat_sec=43.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.8|label=Naka-Hikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)}} '''Naka-Hikawa Shrine''' (なかひかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} of {{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596}} in {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi (Tokorozawa)|Q11466558|WD=Q11466558|lt=Yamaguchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}. It was the general guardian of 92 villages in former {{ill|lt=Iruma District|Q1153801|WD=Q1153801}} and {{ill|lt=Tama District|Q11430789|WD=Q11430789}}, and was a former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}}. === Overview === * It is a candidate shrine for Naka-Hikawa-sha, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}. Like Ōmiya {{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633}}, it enshrines three deities: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}}. * "Naka" in Naka-Hikawa is explained as: midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and {{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942}} ({{ill|lt=Okutama|Q598626|WD=Q598626}}, {{ill|lt=Nishitama District|Q1048689|WD=Q1048689}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}), or midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and the {{ill|lt=provincial capital|Q1629088|WD=Q1629088}} of {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}} ({{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū}}). * Locally it has long been called "Hikawa-sama." === History === * Traditionally founded in the reign of Emperor Sujin. * In late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Ietsugu|Q11466765|WD=Q11466765}} built shrine buildings. They later burned in warfare, but in the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} era (1573-1593), {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Takatada|Q11467543|WD=Q11467543}} restored it together with {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Castle (Musashi)|Q11466729|WD=Q11466729|lt=Yamaguchi Castle}}. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 20 ({{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240}}), Brigadier General Ken R. Dyke, first director of GHQ/CIE, inspected this shrine's festival. ===Shrine buildings / precinct=== *Honden: {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874}}, built in 1927. *Shichisha Shrine: moved into the precincts from an area scheduled to be submerged by {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Reservoir|Q11467481|WD=Q11467481}}. ===Festivals=== *Spring grand festival is on the first Sunday of April; autumn grand festival is on Sports Day in October. ===Location / transportation=== *Saitama Prefecture, Tokorozawa, Yamaguchi 1849 **15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shimo-Yamaguchi Station|Q7496941|WD=Q7496941}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Sayama Line|Q1965549|WD=Q1965549}} ===Managed / served shrines=== *Managed shrines **{{ill|lt=Sengen Shrine (Tokorozawa)||WD=|lt=Sengen Shrine}} (Arahata Fuji) **{{ill|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine (Tokorozawa)||WD=|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine}} *Served shrine (outside precinct) **{{ill|lt=Atago Gongen-sha (Tokorozawa)|Q17191381|WD=Q17191381|lt=Atago Gongen-sha}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943}}|volume=Vol. 158, Iruma District No.3|chapter=Hikawa village, Hikawa shrine|id={{NDLJP|764001/72}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} ===External links=== *[http://nakahikawa.or.jp/ Naka-Hikawa Shrine homepage] ===See also=== *{{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596}} *{{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633}} *{{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942}} *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334}} *{{ill|lt=Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)|Q17226968|WD=Q17226968}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかひかわしんしや ところさわし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の氷川神社]] [[Category:所沢市の歴史]] [[Category:所沢市の建築物]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857}}Yamaguchi 1849 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small) candidate shrine
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874}} |別名 = |例祭 = First Sunday of April, and Sports Day in October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=46|lat_sec=43.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.8|label=Naka-Hikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)}} '''Naka-Hikawa Shrine''' (なかひかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} of {{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596}} in {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi (Tokorozawa)|Q11466558|WD=Q11466558|lt=Yamaguchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}. It was the general guardian of 92 villages in former {{ill|lt=Iruma District|Q1153801|WD=Q1153801}} and {{ill|lt=Tama District|Q11430789|WD=Q11430789}}, and was a former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Overview === * It is a candidate shrine for Naka-Hikawa-sha, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}. Like Ōmiya {{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633}}, it enshrines three deities: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}}. * "Naka" in Naka-Hikawa is explained as: midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and {{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942}} ({{ill|lt=Okutama|Q598626|WD=Q598626}}, {{ill|lt=Nishitama District|Q1048689|WD=Q1048689}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}), or midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and the {{ill|lt=provincial capital|Q1629088|WD=Q1629088}} of {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}} ({{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū}}). * Locally it has long been called "Hikawa-sama." === History === * Traditionally founded in the reign of Emperor Sujin. * In late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Ietsugu|Q11466765|WD=Q11466765}} built shrine buildings. They later burned in warfare, but in the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} era (1573-1593), {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Takatada|Q11467543|WD=Q11467543}} restored it together with {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Castle (Musashi)|Q11466729|WD=Q11466729|lt=Yamaguchi Castle}}. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 20 ({{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240}}), Brigadier General Ken R. Dyke, first director of GHQ/CIE, inspected this shrine's festival. ===Shrine buildings / precinct=== *Honden: {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874}}, built in 1927. *Shichisha Shrine: moved into the precincts from an area scheduled to be submerged by {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Reservoir|Q11467481|WD=Q11467481}}. ===Festivals=== *Spring grand festival is on the first Sunday of April; autumn grand festival is on Sports Day in October. ===Location / transportation=== *Saitama Prefecture, Tokorozawa, Yamaguchi 1849 **15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shimo-Yamaguchi Station|Q7496941|WD=Q7496941}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Sayama Line|Q1965549|WD=Q1965549}} ===Managed / served shrines=== *Managed shrines **{{ill|lt=Sengen Shrine (Tokorozawa)||WD=|lt=Sengen Shrine}} (Arahata Fuji) **{{ill|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine (Tokorozawa)||WD=|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine}} *Served shrine (outside precinct) **{{ill|lt=Atago Gongen-sha (Tokorozawa)|Q17191381|WD=Q17191381|lt=Atago Gongen-sha}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943}}|volume=Vol. 158, Iruma District No.3|chapter=Hikawa village, Hikawa shrine|id={{NDLJP|764001/72}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} ===External links=== *[http://nakahikawa.or.jp/ Naka-Hikawa Shrine homepage] ===See also=== *{{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596}} *{{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633}} *{{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942}} *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334}} *{{ill|lt=Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)|Q17226968|WD=Q17226968}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかひかわしんしや ところさわし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の氷川神社]] [[Category:所沢市の歴史]] [[Category:所沢市の建築物]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857}}Yamaguchi 1849 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small) candidate shrine
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874}} |別名 = |例祭 = First Sunday of April, and Sports Day in October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=46|lat_sec=43.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.8|label=Naka-Hikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)}} '''Naka-Hikawa Shrine''' (なかひかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} of {{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596}} in {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi (Tokorozawa)|Q11466558|WD=Q11466558|lt=Yamaguchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}. It was the general guardian of 92 villages in former {{ill|lt=Iruma District|Q1153801|WD=Q1153801}} and {{ill|lt=Tama District|Q11430789|WD=Q11430789}}, and was a former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Overview === * It is a candidate shrine for Naka-Hikawa-sha, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}. Like Ōmiya {{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633}}, it enshrines three deities: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}}. * "Naka" in Naka-Hikawa is explained as: midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and {{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942}} ({{ill|lt=Okutama|Q598626|WD=Q598626}}, {{ill|lt=Nishitama District|Q1048689|WD=Q1048689}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}), or midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and the {{ill|lt=provincial capital|Q1629088|WD=Q1629088}} of {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}} ({{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū}}). * Locally it has long been called "Hikawa-sama." === History === * Traditionally founded in the reign of Emperor Sujin. * In late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Ietsugu|Q11466765|WD=Q11466765}} built shrine buildings. They later burned in warfare, but in the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} era (1573-1593), {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Takatada|Q11467543|WD=Q11467543}} restored it together with {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Castle (Musashi)|Q11466729|WD=Q11466729|lt=Yamaguchi Castle}}. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 20 ({{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240}}), Brigadier General Ken R. Dyke, first director of GHQ/CIE, inspected this shrine's festival. ===Shrine buildings / precinct=== *Honden: {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874}}, built in 1927. *Shichisha Shrine: moved into the precincts from an area scheduled to be submerged by {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Reservoir|Q11467481|WD=Q11467481}}. ===Festivals=== *Spring grand festival is on the first Sunday of April; autumn grand festival is on Sports Day in October. ===Location / transportation=== *Saitama Prefecture, Tokorozawa, Yamaguchi 1849 **15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shimo-Yamaguchi Station|Q7496941|WD=Q7496941}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Sayama Line|Q1965549|WD=Q1965549}} ===Managed / served shrines=== *Managed shrines **{{ill|lt=Sengen Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Sengen Shrine}} (Arahata Fuji) **{{ill|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine}} *Served shrine (outside precinct) **{{ill|lt=Atago Gongen-sha (Tokorozawa)|Q17191381|WD=Q17191381|lt=Atago Gongen-sha}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943}}|volume=Vol. 158, Iruma District No.3|chapter=Hikawa village, Hikawa shrine|id={{NDLJP|764001/72}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} ===External links=== *[http://nakahikawa.or.jp/ Naka-Hikawa Shrine homepage] ===See also=== *{{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596}} *{{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633}} *{{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942}} *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334}} *{{ill|lt=Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)|Q17226968|WD=Q17226968}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかひかわしんしや ところさわし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の氷川神社]] [[Category:所沢市の歴史]] [[Category:所沢市の建築物]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857}}Yamaguchi 1849 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small) candidate shrine
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874}} |別名 = |例祭 = First Sunday of April, and Sports Day in October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=46|lat_sec=43.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.8|label=Naka-Hikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)}} '''Naka-Hikawa Shrine''' (なかひかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} of {{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596}} in {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi (Tokorozawa)|Q11466558|WD=Q11466558|lt=Yamaguchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}. It was the general guardian of 92 villages in former {{ill|lt=Iruma District|Q1153801|WD=Q1153801}} and {{ill|lt=Tama District|Q11430789|WD=Q11430789}}, and was a former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Overview === * It is a candidate shrine for Naka-Hikawa-sha, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}. Like Ōmiya {{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633}}, it enshrines three deities: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}}. * "Naka" in Naka-Hikawa is explained as: midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and {{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942}} ({{ill|lt=Okutama|Q598626|WD=Q598626}}, {{ill|lt=Nishitama District|Q1048689|WD=Q1048689}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}), or midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and the {{ill|lt=provincial capital|Q1629088|WD=Q1629088}} of {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}} ({{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū}}). * Locally it has long been called "Hikawa-sama." === History === * Traditionally founded in the reign of Emperor Sujin. * In late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Ietsugu|Q11466765|WD=Q11466765}} built shrine buildings. They later burned in warfare, but in the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} era (1573-1593), {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Takatada|Q11467543|WD=Q11467543}} restored it together with {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Castle (Musashi)|Q11466729|WD=Q11466729|lt=Yamaguchi Castle}}. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 20 ({{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240}}), Brigadier General Ken R. Dyke, first director of GHQ/CIE, inspected this shrine's festival. ===Shrine buildings / precinct=== *Honden: {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874}}, built in 1927. *Shichisha Shrine: moved into the precincts from an area scheduled to be submerged by {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Reservoir|Q11467481|WD=Q11467481}}. ===Festivals=== *Spring grand festival is on the first Sunday of April; autumn grand festival is on Sports Day in October. ===Location / transportation=== *Saitama Prefecture, Tokorozawa, Yamaguchi 1849 **15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shimo-Yamaguchi Station|Q7496941|WD=Q7496941}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Sayama Line|Q1965549|WD=Q1965549}} ===Managed / served shrines=== *Managed shrines **{{ill|lt=Sengen Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sengen Shrine}} (Arahata Fuji) **{{ill|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine}} *Served shrine (outside precinct) **{{ill|lt=Atago Gongen-sha (Tokorozawa)|Q17191381|WD=Q17191381|lt=Atago Gongen-sha}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943}}|volume=Vol. 158, Iruma District No.3|chapter=Hikawa village, Hikawa shrine|id={{NDLJP|764001/72}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} ===External links=== *[http://nakahikawa.or.jp/ Naka-Hikawa Shrine homepage] ===See also=== *{{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596}} *{{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633}} *{{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942}} *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334}} *{{ill|lt=Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)|Q17226968|WD=Q17226968}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかひかわしんしや ところさわし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の氷川神社]] [[Category:所沢市の歴史]] [[Category:所沢市の建築物]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857}}Yamaguchi 1849 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}

{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small) candidate shrine
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874}} |別名 = |例祭 = First Sunday of April, and Sports Day in October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=46|lat_sec=43.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.8|label=Naka-Hikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)}} '''Naka-Hikawa Shrine''' (なかひかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} of {{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596}} in {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi (Tokorozawa)|Q11466558|WD=Q11466558|lt=Yamaguchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}. It was the general guardian of 92 villages in former {{ill|lt=Iruma District|Q1153801|WD=Q1153801}} and {{ill|lt=Tama District|Q11430789|WD=Q11430789}}, and was a former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Overview === * It is a candidate shrine for Naka-Hikawa-sha, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}. Like Ōmiya {{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633}}, it enshrines three deities: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}}. * "Naka" in Naka-Hikawa is explained as: midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and {{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942}} ({{ill|lt=Okutama|Q598626|WD=Q598626}}, {{ill|lt=Nishitama District|Q1048689|WD=Q1048689}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}), or midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and the {{ill|lt=provincial capital|Q1629088|WD=Q1629088}} of {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}} ({{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū}}). * Locally it has long been called "Hikawa-sama." === History === * Traditionally founded in the reign of Emperor Sujin. * In late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Ietsugu|Q11466765|WD=Q11466765}} built shrine buildings. They later burned in warfare, but in the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} era (1573-1593), {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Takatada|Q11467543|WD=Q11467543}} restored it together with {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Castle (Musashi)|Q11466729|WD=Q11466729|lt=Yamaguchi Castle}}. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 20 ({{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240}}), Brigadier General Ken R. Dyke, first director of GHQ/CIE, inspected this shrine's festival. ===Shrine buildings / precinct=== *Honden: {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874}}, built in 1927. *Shichisha Shrine: moved into the precincts from an area scheduled to be submerged by {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Reservoir|Q11467481|WD=Q11467481}}. ===Festivals=== *Spring grand festival is on the first Sunday of April; autumn grand festival is on Sports Day in October. ===Location / transportation=== *Saitama Prefecture, Tokorozawa, Yamaguchi 1849 **15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shimo-Yamaguchi Station|Q7496941|WD=Q7496941}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Sayama Line|Q1965549|WD=Q1965549}} ===Managed / served shrines=== *Managed shrines **{{ill|lt=Sengen Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sengen Shrine}} (Arahata Fuji) **{{ill|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine}} *Served shrine (outside precinct) **{{ill|lt=Atago Gongen-sha (Tokorozawa)|Q17191381|WD=Q17191381|lt=Atago Gongen-sha}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943}}|volume=Vol. 158, Iruma District No.3|chapter=Hikawa village, Hikawa shrine|id={{NDLJP|764001/72}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} ===External links=== *[http://nakahikawa.or.jp/ Naka-Hikawa Shrine homepage] ===See also=== *{{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596}} *{{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633}} *{{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942}} *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334}} *{{ill|lt=Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)|Q17226968|WD=Q17226968}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかひかわしんしや ところさわし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の氷川神社]] [[Category:所沢市の歴史]] [[Category:所沢市の建築物]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874}} |別名 = |例祭 = First Sunday of April, and Sports Day in October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=46|lat_sec=43.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.8|label=Naka-Hikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)}} '''Naka-Hikawa Shrine''' (なかひかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} of {{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596}} in {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi (Tokorozawa)|Q11466558|WD=Q11466558|lt=Yamaguchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}. It was the general guardian of 92 villages in former {{ill|lt=Iruma District|Q1153801|WD=Q1153801}} and {{ill|lt=Tama District|Q11430789|WD=Q11430789}}, and was a former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Overview === * It is a candidate shrine for Naka-Hikawa-sha, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}. Like Ōmiya {{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633}}, it enshrines three deities: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}}. * "Naka" in Naka-Hikawa is explained as: midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and {{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942}} ({{ill|lt=Okutama|Q598626|WD=Q598626}}, {{ill|lt=Nishitama District|Q1048689|WD=Q1048689}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}), or midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and the {{ill|lt=provincial capital|Q1629088|WD=Q1629088}} of {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}} ({{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū}}). * Locally it has long been called "Hikawa-sama." === History === * Traditionally founded in the reign of Emperor Sujin. * In late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Ietsugu|Q11466765|WD=Q11466765}} built shrine buildings. They later burned in warfare, but in the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} era (1573-1593), {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Takatada|Q11467543|WD=Q11467543}} restored it together with {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Castle (Musashi)|Q11466729|WD=Q11466729|lt=Yamaguchi Castle}}. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 20 ({{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240}}), Brigadier General Ken R. Dyke, first director of GHQ/CIE, inspected this shrine's festival. ===Shrine buildings / precinct=== *Honden: {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874}}, built in 1927. *Shichisha Shrine: moved into the precincts from an area scheduled to be submerged by {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Reservoir|Q11467481|WD=Q11467481}}. ===Festivals=== *Spring grand festival is on the first Sunday of April; autumn grand festival is on Sports Day in October. ===Location / transportation=== *Saitama Prefecture, Tokorozawa, Yamaguchi 1849 **15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shimo-Yamaguchi Station|Q7496941|WD=Q7496941}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Sayama Line|Q1965549|WD=Q1965549}} ===Managed / served shrines=== *Managed shrines **{{ill|lt=Sengen Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sengen Shrine}} (Arahata Fuji) **{{ill|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine}} *Served shrine (outside precinct) **{{ill|lt=Atago Gongen-sha (Tokorozawa)|Q17191381|WD=Q17191381|lt=Atago Gongen-sha}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943}}|volume=Vol. 158, Iruma District No.3|chapter=Hikawa village, Hikawa shrine|id={{NDLJP|764001/72}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} ===External links=== *[http://nakahikawa.or.jp/ Naka-Hikawa Shrine homepage] ===See also=== *{{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596}} *{{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633}} *{{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942}} *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334}} *{{ill|lt=Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)|Q17226968|WD=Q17226968}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかひかわしんしや ところさわし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の氷川神社]] [[Category:所沢市の歴史]] [[Category:所沢市の建築物]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small) candidate shrine
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874}} |別名 = |例祭 = First Sunday of April, and Sports Day in October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=46|lat_sec=43.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.8|label=Naka-Hikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)}} '''Naka-Hikawa Shrine''' (なかひかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} of {{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596}} in {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi (Tokorozawa)|Q11466558|WD=Q11466558|lt=Yamaguchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}. It was the general guardian of 92 villages in former {{ill|lt=Iruma District|Q1153801|WD=Q1153801}} and {{ill|lt=Tama District|Q11430789|WD=Q11430789}}, and was a former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Overview === * It is a candidate shrine for Naka-Hikawa-sha, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}. Like Ōmiya {{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633}}, it enshrines three deities: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}}. * "Naka" in Naka-Hikawa is explained as: midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and {{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942}} ({{ill|lt=Okutama|Q598626|WD=Q598626}}, {{ill|lt=Nishitama District|Q1048689|WD=Q1048689}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}), or midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and the {{ill|lt=provincial capital|Q1629088|WD=Q1629088}} of {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}} ({{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū}}). * Locally it has long been called "Hikawa-sama." === History === * Traditionally founded in the reign of Emperor Sujin. * In late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Ietsugu|Q11466765|WD=Q11466765}} built shrine buildings. They later burned in warfare, but in the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} era (1573-1593), {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Takatada|Q11467543|WD=Q11467543}} restored it together with {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Castle (Musashi)|Q11466729|WD=Q11466729|lt=Yamaguchi Castle}}. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 20 ({{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240}}), Brigadier General Ken R. Dyke, first director of GHQ/CIE, inspected this shrine's festival. ===Shrine buildings / precinct=== *Honden: {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874}}, built in 1927. *Shichisha Shrine: moved into the precincts from an area scheduled to be submerged by {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Reservoir|Q11467481|WD=Q11467481}}. ===Festivals=== *Spring grand festival is on the first Sunday of April; autumn grand festival is on Sports Day in October. ===Location / transportation=== *Saitama Prefecture, Tokorozawa, Yamaguchi 1849 **15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shimo-Yamaguchi Station|Q7496941|WD=Q7496941}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Sayama Line|Q1965549|WD=Q1965549}} ===Managed / served shrines=== *Managed shrines **{{ill|lt=Sengen Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sengen Shrine}} (Arahata Fuji) **{{ill|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine}} *Served shrine (outside precinct) **{{ill|lt=Atago Gongen-sha (Tokorozawa)|Q17191381|WD=Q17191381|lt=Atago Gongen-sha}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943}}|volume=Vol. 158, Iruma District No.3|chapter=Hikawa village, Hikawa shrine|id={{NDLJP|764001/72}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} ===External links=== *[http://nakahikawa.or.jp/ Naka-Hikawa Shrine homepage] ===See also=== *{{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596}} *{{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633}} *{{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942}} *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334}} *{{ill|lt=Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)|Q17226968|WD=Q17226968}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかひかわしんしや ところさわし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の氷川神社]] [[Category:所沢市の歴史]] [[Category:所沢市の建築物]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857}}Yamaguchi 1849 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993}}

{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small) candidate shrine
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874}} |別名 = |例祭 = First Sunday of April, and Sports Day in October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=46|lat_sec=43.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.8|label=Naka-Hikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)}} '''Naka-Hikawa Shrine''' (なかひかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} of {{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596}} in {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi (Tokorozawa)|Q11466558|WD=Q11466558|lt=Yamaguchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}. It was the general guardian of 92 villages in former {{ill|lt=Iruma District|Q1153801|WD=Q1153801}} and {{ill|lt=Tama District|Q11430789|WD=Q11430789}}, and was a former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}}. === Overview === * It is a candidate shrine for Naka-Hikawa-sha, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}. Like Ōmiya {{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633}}, it enshrines three deities: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}}. * "Naka" in Naka-Hikawa is explained as: midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and {{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942}} ({{ill|lt=Okutama|Q598626|WD=Q598626}}, {{ill|lt=Nishitama District|Q1048689|WD=Q1048689}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}), or midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and the {{ill|lt=provincial capital|Q1629088|WD=Q1629088}} of {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}} ({{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū}}). * Locally it has long been called "Hikawa-sama." === History === * Traditionally founded in the reign of Emperor Sujin. * In late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Ietsugu|Q11466765|WD=Q11466765}} built shrine buildings. They later burned in warfare, but in the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} era (1573-1593), {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Takatada|Q11467543|WD=Q11467543}} restored it together with {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Castle (Musashi)|Q11466729|WD=Q11466729|lt=Yamaguchi Castle}}. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 20 ({{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240}}), Brigadier General Ken R. Dyke, first director of GHQ/CIE, inspected this shrine's festival. ===Shrine buildings / precinct=== *Honden: {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874}}, built in 1927. *Shichisha Shrine: moved into the precincts from an area scheduled to be submerged by {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Reservoir|Q11467481|WD=Q11467481}}. ===Festivals=== *Spring grand festival is on the first Sunday of April; autumn grand festival is on Sports Day in October. ===Location / transportation=== *Saitama Prefecture, Tokorozawa, Yamaguchi 1849 **15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shimo-Yamaguchi Station|Q7496941|WD=Q7496941}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Sayama Line|Q1965549|WD=Q1965549}} ===Managed / served shrines=== *Managed shrines **{{ill|lt=Sengen Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sengen Shrine}} (Arahata Fuji) **{{ill|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine}} *Served shrine (outside precinct) **{{ill|lt=Atago Gongen-sha (Tokorozawa)|Q17191381|WD=Q17191381|lt=Atago Gongen-sha}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943}}|volume=Vol. 158, Iruma District No.3|chapter=Hikawa village, Hikawa shrine|id={{NDLJP|764001/72}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} ===External links=== *[http://nakahikawa.or.jp/ Naka-Hikawa Shrine homepage] ===See also=== *{{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596}} *{{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633}} *{{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942}} *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334}} *{{ill|lt=Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)|Q17226968|WD=Q17226968}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかひかわしんしや ところさわし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の氷川神社]] [[Category:所沢市の歴史]] [[Category:所沢市の建築物]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857}}Yamaguchi 1849 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993}}

{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small) candidate shrine
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874}} |別名 = |例祭 = First Sunday of April, and Sports Day in October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=46|lat_sec=43.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.8|label=Naka-Hikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)}} '''Naka-Hikawa Shrine''' (なかひかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} of {{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596}} in {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi (Tokorozawa)|Q11466558|WD=Q11466558|lt=Yamaguchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}. It was the general guardian of 92 villages in former {{ill|lt=Iruma District|Q1153801|WD=Q1153801}} and {{ill|lt=Tama District|Q11430789|WD=Q11430789}}, and was a former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}}. === Overview === * It is a candidate shrine for Naka-Hikawa-sha, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}. Like Ōmiya {{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633}}, it enshrines three deities: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}}. * "Naka" in Naka-Hikawa is explained as: midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and {{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942}} ({{ill|lt=Okutama|Q598626|WD=Q598626}}, {{ill|lt=Nishitama District|Q1048689|WD=Q1048689}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}), or midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and the {{ill|lt=provincial capital|Q1629088|WD=Q1629088}} of {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}} ({{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū}}). * Locally it has long been called "Hikawa-sama." === History === * Traditionally founded in the reign of Emperor Sujin. * In late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Ietsugu|Q11466765|WD=Q11466765}} built shrine buildings. They later burned in warfare, but in the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} era (1573-1593), {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Takatada|Q11467543|WD=Q11467543}} restored it together with {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Castle (Musashi)|Q11466729|WD=Q11466729|lt=Yamaguchi Castle}}. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 20 ({{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240}}), Brigadier General Ken R. Dyke, first director of GHQ/CIE, inspected this shrine's festival. ===Shrine buildings / precinct=== *Honden: {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874}}, built in 1927. *Shichisha Shrine: moved into the precincts from an area scheduled to be submerged by {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Reservoir|Q11467481|WD=Q11467481}}. ===Festivals=== *Spring grand festival is on the first Sunday of April; autumn grand festival is on Sports Day in October. ===Location / transportation=== *Saitama Prefecture, Tokorozawa, Yamaguchi 1849 **15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shimo-Yamaguchi Station|Q7496941|WD=Q7496941}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Sayama Line|Q1965549|WD=Q1965549}} ===Managed / served shrines=== *Managed shrines **{{ill|lt=Sengen Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sengen Shrine}} (Arahata Fuji) **{{ill|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine}} *Served shrine (outside precinct) **{{ill|lt=Atago Gongen-sha (Tokorozawa)|Q17191381|WD=Q17191381|lt=Atago Gongen-sha}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943}}|volume=Vol. 158, Iruma District No.3|chapter=Hikawa village, Hikawa shrine|id={{NDLJP|764001/72}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} ===External links=== *[http://nakahikawa.or.jp/ Naka-Hikawa Shrine homepage] ===See also=== *{{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596}} *{{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633}} *{{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942}} *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334}} *{{ill|lt=Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)|Q17226968|WD=Q17226968}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかひかわしんしや ところさわし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の氷川神社]] [[Category:所沢市の歴史]] [[Category:所沢市の建築物]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]
