{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small) candidate shrine
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874}} |別名 = |例祭 = First Sunday of April, and Sports Day in October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=46|lat_sec=43.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.8|label=Naka-Hikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)}} '''Naka-Hikawa Shrine''' (なかひかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} of {{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596}} in {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi (Tokorozawa)|Q11466558|WD=Q11466558|lt=Yamaguchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}. It was the general guardian of 92 villages in former {{ill|lt=Iruma District|Q1153801|WD=Q1153801}} and {{ill|lt=Tama District|Q11430789|WD=Q11430789}}, and was a former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}}. === Overview === * It is a candidate shrine for Naka-Hikawa-sha, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}. Like Ōmiya {{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633}}, it enshrines three deities: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}}. * "Naka" in Naka-Hikawa is explained as: midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and {{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942}} ({{ill|lt=Okutama|Q598626|WD=Q598626}}, {{ill|lt=Nishitama District|Q1048689|WD=Q1048689}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}), or midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and the {{ill|lt=provincial capital|Q1629088|WD=Q1629088}} of {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}} ({{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū}}). * Locally it has long been called "Hikawa-sama." === History === * Traditionally founded in the reign of Emperor Sujin. * In late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Ietsugu|Q11466765|WD=Q11466765}} built shrine buildings. They later burned in warfare, but in the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} era (1573-1593), {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Takatada|Q11467543|WD=Q11467543}} restored it together with {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Castle (Musashi)|Q11466729|WD=Q11466729|lt=Yamaguchi Castle}}. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 20 ({{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240}}), Brigadier General Ken R. Dyke, first director of GHQ/CIE, inspected this shrine's festival. ===Shrine buildings / precinct=== *Honden: {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874}}, built in 1927. *Shichisha Shrine: moved into the precincts from an area scheduled to be submerged by {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Reservoir|Q11467481|WD=Q11467481}}. ===Festivals=== *Spring grand festival is on the first Sunday of April; autumn grand festival is on Sports Day in October. ===Location / transportation=== *Saitama Prefecture, Tokorozawa, Yamaguchi 1849 **15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shimo-Yamaguchi Station|Q7496941|WD=Q7496941}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Sayama Line|Q1965549|WD=Q1965549}} ===Managed / served shrines=== *Managed shrines **{{ill|lt=Sengen Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sengen Shrine}} (Arahata Fuji) **{{ill|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine}} *Served shrine (outside precinct) **{{ill|lt=Atago Gongen-sha (Tokorozawa)|Q17191381|WD=Q17191381|lt=Atago Gongen-sha}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943}}|volume=Vol. 158, Iruma District No.3|chapter=Hikawa village, Hikawa shrine|id={{NDLJP|764001/72}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} ===External links=== *[http://nakahikawa.or.jp/ Naka-Hikawa Shrine homepage] ===See also=== *{{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596}} *{{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633}} *{{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942}} *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334}} *{{ill|lt=Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)|Q17226968|WD=Q17226968}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかひかわしんしや ところさわし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の氷川神社]] [[Category:所沢市の歴史]] [[Category:所沢市の建築物]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== * {'text': '埼玉県所沢市山口1850', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Naka-hikawa-jinja (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)}} [[JA:中氷川神社 (所沢市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11366320}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|Naka-Hikawa Shrine in Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa|Naka-Hikawa Shrine in Mikajima, Tokorozawa|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)}} {{神社 |名称 = Naka-Hikawa Shrine |画像 = [[File:Naka-Hikawa-jinja (Tokorozawa Yamaguchi) 01.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857}}Yamaguchi 1849 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small) candidate shrine
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874}} |別名 = |例祭 = First Sunday of April, and Sports Day in October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=46|lat_sec=43.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.8|label=Naka-Hikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)}} '''Naka-Hikawa Shrine''' (なかひかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} of {{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596}} in {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi (Tokorozawa)|Q11466558|WD=Q11466558|lt=Yamaguchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}. It was the general guardian of 92 villages in former {{ill|lt=Iruma District|Q1153801|WD=Q1153801}} and {{ill|lt=Tama District|Q11430789|WD=Q11430789}}, and was a former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}}. === Overview === * It is a candidate shrine for Naka-Hikawa-sha, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}. Like Ōmiya {{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633}}, it enshrines three deities: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}}. * "Naka" in Naka-Hikawa is explained as: midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and {{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942}} ({{ill|lt=Okutama|Q598626|WD=Q598626}}, {{ill|lt=Nishitama District|Q1048689|WD=Q1048689}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}), or midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and the {{ill|lt=provincial capital|Q1629088|WD=Q1629088}} of {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}} ({{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū}}). * Locally it has long been called "Hikawa-sama." === History === * Traditionally founded in the reign of Emperor Sujin. * In late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Ietsugu|Q11466765|WD=Q11466765}} built shrine buildings. They later burned in warfare, but in the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} era (1573-1593), {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Takatada|Q11467543|WD=Q11467543}} restored it together with {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Castle (Musashi)|Q11466729|WD=Q11466729|lt=Yamaguchi Castle}}. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 20 ({{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240}}), Brigadier General Ken R. Dyke, first director of GHQ/CIE, inspected this shrine's festival. ===Shrine buildings / precinct=== *Honden: {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874}}, built in 1927. *Shichisha Shrine: moved into the precincts from an area scheduled to be submerged by {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Reservoir|Q11467481|WD=Q11467481}}. ===Festivals=== *Spring grand festival is on the first Sunday of April; autumn grand festival is on Sports Day in October. ===Location / transportation=== *Saitama Prefecture, Tokorozawa, Yamaguchi 1849 **15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shimo-Yamaguchi Station|Q7496941|WD=Q7496941}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Sayama Line|Q1965549|WD=Q1965549}} ===Managed / served shrines=== *Managed shrines **{{ill|lt=Sengen Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sengen Shrine}} (Arahata Fuji) **{{ill|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine}} *Served shrine (outside precinct) **{{ill|lt=Atago Gongen-sha (Tokorozawa)|Q17191381|WD=Q17191381|lt=Atago Gongen-sha}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943}}|volume=Vol. 158, Iruma District No.3|chapter=Hikawa village, Hikawa shrine|id={{NDLJP|764001/72}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} ===External links=== *[http://nakahikawa.or.jp/ Naka-Hikawa Shrine homepage] ===See also=== *{{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596}} *{{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633}} *{{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942}} *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334}} *{{ill|lt=Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)|Q17226968|WD=Q17226968}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかひかわしんしや ところさわし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の氷川神社]] [[Category:所沢市の歴史]] [[Category:所沢市の建築物]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)}} {{神社 |名称 = Naka-Hikawa Shrine |画像 = [[File:Naka-Hikawa-jinja (Tokorozawa Yamaguchi) 01.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}Yamaguchi 1849 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}

{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) candidate shrine
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = First Sunday of April, and Sports Day in October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=46|lat_sec=43.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.8|label=Naka-Hikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)}} '''Naka-Hikawa Shrine''' (なかひかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596|1=Hikawa shinkō}} in {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi (Tokorozawa)|Q11466558|WD=Q11466558|lt=Yamaguchi|1=Yamaguchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It was the general guardian of 92 villages in former {{ill|lt=Iruma District|Q1153801|WD=Q1153801|1=Iruma District, Saitama}} and {{ill|lt=Tama District|Q11430789|WD=Q11430789|1=Tama district}}, and was a former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Overview === * It is a candidate shrine for Naka-Hikawa-sha, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Like Ōmiya {{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633|1=Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)}}, it enshrines three deities: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}, and {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}. * "Naka" in Naka-Hikawa is explained as: midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and {{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942|1=Oku-Hikawa Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Okutama|Q598626|WD=Q598626|1=Okutama, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Nishitama District|Q1048689|WD=Q1048689|1=Nishitama District}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}), or midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and the {{ill|lt=provincial capital|Q1629088|WD=Q1629088|1=Kokufu}} of {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}} ({{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}}). * Locally it has long been called "Hikawa-sama." === History === * Traditionally founded in the reign of Emperor Sujin. * In late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Ietsugu|Q11466765|WD=Q11466765|1=Yamaguchi Ietsugu}} built shrine buildings. They later burned in warfare, but in the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era (1573-1593), {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Takatada|Q11467543|WD=Q11467543|1=Yamaguchi Takatada}} restored it together with {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Castle (Musashi)|Q11466729|WD=Q11466729|lt=Yamaguchi Castle|1=Yamaguchi Castle}}. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 20 ({{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}}), Brigadier General Ken R. Dyke, first director of GHQ/CIE, inspected this shrine's festival. ===Shrine buildings / precinct=== *Honden: {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}}, built in 1927. *Shichisha Shrine: moved into the precincts from an area scheduled to be submerged by {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Reservoir|Q11467481|WD=Q11467481|1=Yamaguchi Reservoir}}. ===Festivals=== *Spring grand festival is on the first Sunday of April; autumn grand festival is on Sports Day in October. ===Location / transportation=== *Saitama Prefecture, Tokorozawa, Yamaguchi 1849 **15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shimo-Yamaguchi Station|Q7496941|WD=Q7496941|1=Shimo-Yamaguchi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Sayama Line|Q1965549|WD=Q1965549|1=Seibu Sayama Line}} ===Managed / served shrines=== *Managed shrines **{{ill|lt=Sengen Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sengen Shrine}} (Arahata Fuji) **{{ill|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine}} *Served shrine (outside precinct) **{{ill|lt=Atago Gongen-sha (Tokorozawa)|Q17191381|WD=Q17191381|lt=Atago Gongen-sha|1=Atago Daigongen Shrine}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}|volume=Vol. 158, Iruma District No.3|chapter=Hikawa village, Hikawa shrine|id={{NDLJP|764001/72}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} ===External links=== *[http://nakahikawa.or.jp/ Naka-Hikawa Shrine homepage] ===See also=== *{{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596|1=Hikawa shinkō}} *{{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633|1=Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)}} *{{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942|1=Oku-Hikawa Shrine}} *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334|1=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province}} *{{ill|lt=Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)|Q17226968|WD=Q17226968|1=Nakahikawa Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかひかわしんしや ところさわし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の氷川神社]] [[Category:所沢市の歴史]] [[Category:所沢市の建築物]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Tokorozawa)}} [[JA:中氷川神社 (所沢市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11366320}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|Naka-Hikawa Shrine in Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa|Naka-Hikawa Shrine in Mikajima, Tokorozawa|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)}} {{神社 |名称 = Naka-Hikawa Shrine |画像 = [[File:Naka-Hikawa-jinja (Tokorozawa Yamaguchi) 01.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}Yamaguchi 1849 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}
{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) candidate shrine
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = First Sunday of April, and Sports Day in October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=46|lat_sec=43.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.8|label=Naka-Hikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)}} '''Naka-Hikawa Shrine''' (なかひかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596|1=Hikawa shinkō}} in {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi (Tokorozawa)|Q11466558|WD=Q11466558|lt=Yamaguchi|1=Yamaguchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It was the general guardian of 92 villages in former {{ill|lt=Iruma District|Q1153801|WD=Q1153801|1=Iruma District, Saitama}} and {{ill|lt=Tama District|Q11430789|WD=Q11430789|1=Tama district}}, and was a former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Overview === * It is a candidate shrine for Naka-Hikawa-sha, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Like Ōmiya {{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633|1=Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)}}, it enshrines three deities: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}, and {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}. * "Naka" in Naka-Hikawa is explained as: midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and {{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942|1=Oku-Hikawa Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Okutama|Q598626|WD=Q598626|1=Okutama, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Nishitama District|Q1048689|WD=Q1048689|1=Nishitama District}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}), or midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and the {{ill|lt=provincial capital|Q1629088|WD=Q1629088|1=Kokufu}} of {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}} ({{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}}). * Locally it has long been called "Hikawa-sama." === History === * Traditionally founded in the reign of Emperor Sujin. * In late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Ietsugu|Q11466765|WD=Q11466765|1=Yamaguchi Ietsugu}} built shrine buildings. They later burned in warfare, but in the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era (1573-1593), {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Takatada|Q11467543|WD=Q11467543|1=Yamaguchi Takatada}} restored it together with {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Castle (Musashi)|Q11466729|WD=Q11466729|lt=Yamaguchi Castle|1=Yamaguchi Castle}}. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 20 ({{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}}), Brigadier General Ken R. Dyke, first director of GHQ/CIE, inspected this shrine's festival. ===Shrine buildings / precinct=== *Honden: {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}}, built in 1927. *Shichisha Shrine: moved into the precincts from an area scheduled to be submerged by {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Reservoir|Q11467481|WD=Q11467481|1=Yamaguchi Reservoir}}. ===Festivals=== *Spring grand festival is on the first Sunday of April; autumn grand festival is on Sports Day in October. ===Location / transportation=== *Saitama Prefecture, Tokorozawa, Yamaguchi 1849 **15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shimo-Yamaguchi Station|Q7496941|WD=Q7496941|1=Shimo-Yamaguchi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Sayama Line|Q1965549|WD=Q1965549|1=Seibu Sayama Line}} ===Managed / served shrines=== *Managed shrines **{{ill|lt=Sengen Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sengen Shrine}} (Arahata Fuji) **{{ill|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine}} *Served shrine (outside precinct) **{{ill|lt=Atago Gongen-sha (Tokorozawa)|Q17191381|WD=Q17191381|lt=Atago Gongen-sha|1=Atago Daigongen Shrine}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}|volume=Vol. 158, Iruma District No.3|chapter=Hikawa village, Hikawa shrine|id={{NDLJP|764001/72}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} ===External links=== *[http://nakahikawa.or.jp/ Naka-Hikawa Shrine homepage] ===See also=== *{{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596|1=Hikawa shinkō}} *{{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633|1=Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)}} *{{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942|1=Oku-Hikawa Shrine}} *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334|1=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province}} *{{ill|lt=Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)|Q17226968|WD=Q17226968|1=Nakahikawa Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかひかわしんしや ところさわし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の氷川神社]] [[Category:所沢市の歴史]] [[Category:所沢市の建築物]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Tokorozawa)}} [[JA:中氷川神社 (所沢市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11366320}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|Naka-Hikawa Shrine in Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa|Naka-Hikawa Shrine in Mikajima, Tokorozawa|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)}} {{神社 |名称 = Naka-Hikawa Shrine |画像 = [[File:Naka-Hikawa-jinja (Tokorozawa Yamaguchi) 01.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}Yamaguchi 1849 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}
{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) candidate shrine
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = First Sunday of April, and Sports Day in October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=46|lat_sec=43.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.8|label=Naka-Hikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)}} '''Naka-Hikawa Shrine''' (なかひかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596|1=Hikawa shinkō}} in {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi (Tokorozawa)|Q11466558|WD=Q11466558|lt=Yamaguchi|1=Yamaguchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It was the general guardian of 92 villages in former {{ill|lt=Iruma District|Q1153801|WD=Q1153801|1=Iruma District, Saitama}} and {{ill|lt=Tama District|Q11430789|WD=Q11430789|1=Tama district}}, and was a former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Overview === * It is a candidate shrine for Naka-Hikawa-sha, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Like Ōmiya {{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633|1=Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)}}, it enshrines three deities: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}, and {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}. * "Naka" in Naka-Hikawa is explained as: midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and {{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942|1=Oku-Hikawa Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Okutama|Q598626|WD=Q598626|1=Okutama, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Nishitama District|Q1048689|WD=Q1048689|1=Nishitama District}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}), or midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and the {{ill|lt=provincial capital|Q1629088|WD=Q1629088|1=Kokufu}} of {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}} ({{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}}). * Locally it has long been called "Hikawa-sama." === History === * Traditionally founded in the reign of Emperor Sujin. * In late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Ietsugu|Q11466765|WD=Q11466765|1=Yamaguchi Ietsugu}} built shrine buildings. They later burned in warfare, but in the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era (1573-1593), {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Takatada|Q11467543|WD=Q11467543|1=Yamaguchi Takatada}} restored it together with {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Castle (Musashi)|Q11466729|WD=Q11466729|lt=Yamaguchi Castle|1=Yamaguchi Castle}}. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 20 ({{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}}), Brigadier General Ken R. Dyke, first director of GHQ/CIE, inspected this shrine's festival. ===Shrine buildings / precinct=== *Honden: {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}}, built in 1927. *Shichisha Shrine: moved into the precincts from an area scheduled to be submerged by {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Reservoir|Q11467481|WD=Q11467481|1=Yamaguchi Reservoir}}. ===Festivals=== *Spring grand festival is on the first Sunday of April; autumn grand festival is on Sports Day in October. ===Location / transportation=== *Saitama Prefecture, Tokorozawa, Yamaguchi 1849 **15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shimo-Yamaguchi Station|Q7496941|WD=Q7496941|1=Shimo-Yamaguchi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Sayama Line|Q1965549|WD=Q1965549|1=Seibu Sayama Line}} ===Managed / served shrines=== *Managed shrines **{{ill|lt=Sengen Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sengen Shrine}} (Arahata Fuji) **{{ill|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine}} *Served shrine (outside precinct) **{{ill|lt=Atago Gongen-sha (Tokorozawa)|Q17191381|WD=Q17191381|lt=Atago Gongen-sha|1=Atago Daigongen Shrine}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}|volume=Vol. 158, Iruma District No.3|chapter=Hikawa village, Hikawa shrine|id={{NDLJP|764001/72}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} ===External links=== *[http://nakahikawa.or.jp/ Naka-Hikawa Shrine homepage] ===See also=== *{{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596|1=Hikawa shinkō}} *{{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633|1=Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)}} *{{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942|1=Oku-Hikawa Shrine}} *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334|1=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province}} *{{ill|lt=Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)|Q17226968|WD=Q17226968|1=Nakahikawa Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかひかわしんしや ところさわし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の氷川神社]] [[Category:所沢市の歴史]] [[Category:所沢市の建築物]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]]

{{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '埼玉県所沢市山口1850', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Naka-hikawa-jinja (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)}} [[JA:中氷川神社 (所沢市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11366320}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|Naka-Hikawa Shrine in Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa|Naka-Hikawa Shrine in Mikajima, Tokorozawa|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)}} {{神社 |名称 = Naka-Hikawa Shrine |画像 = [[File:Naka-Hikawa-jinja (Tokorozawa Yamaguchi) 01.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}Yamaguchi 1849 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}
{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) candidate shrine
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = First Sunday of April, and Sports Day in October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=46|lat_sec=43.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.8|label=Naka-Hikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)}} '''Naka-Hikawa Shrine''' (なかひかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596|1=Hikawa shinkō}} in {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi (Tokorozawa)|Q11466558|WD=Q11466558|lt=Yamaguchi|1=Yamaguchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It was the general guardian of 92 villages in former {{ill|lt=Iruma District|Q1153801|WD=Q1153801|1=Iruma District, Saitama}} and {{ill|lt=Tama District|Q11430789|WD=Q11430789|1=Tama district}}, and was a former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Overview === * It is a candidate shrine for Naka-Hikawa-sha, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Like Ōmiya {{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633|1=Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)}}, it enshrines three deities: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}, and {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}. * "Naka" in Naka-Hikawa is explained as: midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and {{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942|1=Oku-Hikawa Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Okutama|Q598626|WD=Q598626|1=Okutama, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Nishitama District|Q1048689|WD=Q1048689|1=Nishitama District}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}), or midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and the {{ill|lt=provincial capital|Q1629088|WD=Q1629088|1=Kokufu}} of {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}} ({{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}}). * Locally it has long been called "Hikawa-sama." === History === * Traditionally founded in the reign of Emperor Sujin. * In late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Ietsugu|Q11466765|WD=Q11466765|1=Yamaguchi Ietsugu}} built shrine buildings. They later burned in warfare, but in the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era (1573-1593), {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Takatada|Q11467543|WD=Q11467543|1=Yamaguchi Takatada}} restored it together with {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Castle (Musashi)|Q11466729|WD=Q11466729|lt=Yamaguchi Castle|1=Yamaguchi Castle}}. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 20 ({{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}}), Brigadier General Ken R. Dyke, first director of GHQ/CIE, inspected this shrine's festival. ===Shrine buildings / precinct=== *Honden: {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}}, built in 1927. *Shichisha Shrine: moved into the precincts from an area scheduled to be submerged by {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Reservoir|Q11467481|WD=Q11467481|1=Yamaguchi Reservoir}}. ===Festivals=== *Spring grand festival is on the first Sunday of April; autumn grand festival is on Sports Day in October. ===Location / transportation=== *Saitama Prefecture, Tokorozawa, Yamaguchi 1849 **15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shimo-Yamaguchi Station|Q7496941|WD=Q7496941|1=Shimo-Yamaguchi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Sayama Line|Q1965549|WD=Q1965549|1=Seibu Sayama Line}} ===Managed / served shrines=== *Managed shrines **{{ill|lt=Sengen Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sengen Shrine}} (Arahata Fuji) **{{ill|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine}} *Served shrine (outside precinct) **{{ill|lt=Atago Gongen-sha (Tokorozawa)|Q17191381|WD=Q17191381|lt=Atago Gongen-sha|1=Atago Daigongen Shrine}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}|volume=Vol. 158, Iruma District No.3|chapter=Hikawa village, Hikawa shrine|id={{NDLJP|764001/72}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} ===External links=== *[http://nakahikawa.or.jp/ Naka-Hikawa Shrine homepage] ===See also=== *{{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596|1=Hikawa shinkō}} *{{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633|1=Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)}} *{{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942|1=Oku-Hikawa Shrine}} *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334|1=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province}} *{{ill|lt=Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)|Q17226968|WD=Q17226968|1=Nakahikawa Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の氷川神社]] [[Category:所沢市の歴史]] [[Category:所沢市の建築物]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]]

Tokorozawa)}} [[JA:中氷川神社 (所沢市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11366320}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|Naka-Hikawa Shrine in Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa|Naka-Hikawa Shrine in Mikajima, Tokorozawa|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)}} {{神社 |名称 = Naka-Hikawa Shrine |画像 = [[File:Naka-Hikawa-jinja (Tokorozawa Yamaguchi) 01.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}Yamaguchi 1849 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}
{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) candidate shrine
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = First Sunday of April, and Sports Day in October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=46|lat_sec=43.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.8|label=Naka-Hikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)}} '''Naka-Hikawa Shrine''' (なかひかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596|1=Hikawa shinkō}} in {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi (Tokorozawa)|Q11466558|WD=Q11466558|lt=Yamaguchi|1=Yamaguchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It was the general guardian of 92 villages in former {{ill|lt=Iruma District|Q1153801|WD=Q1153801|1=Iruma District, Saitama}} and {{ill|lt=Tama District|Q11430789|WD=Q11430789|1=Tama district}}, and was a former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Overview === * It is a candidate shrine for Naka-Hikawa-sha, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Like Ōmiya {{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633|1=Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)}}, it enshrines three deities: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}, and {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}. * "Naka" in Naka-Hikawa is explained as: midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and {{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942|1=Oku-Hikawa Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Okutama|Q598626|WD=Q598626|1=Okutama, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Nishitama District|Q1048689|WD=Q1048689|1=Nishitama District}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}), or midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and the {{ill|lt=provincial capital|Q1629088|WD=Q1629088|1=Kokufu}} of {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}} ({{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}}). * Locally it has long been called "Hikawa-sama." === History === * Traditionally founded in the reign of Emperor Sujin. * In late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Ietsugu|Q11466765|WD=Q11466765|1=Yamaguchi Ietsugu}} built shrine buildings. They later burned in warfare, but in the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era (1573-1593), {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Takatada|Q11467543|WD=Q11467543|1=Yamaguchi Takatada}} restored it together with {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Castle (Musashi)|Q11466729|WD=Q11466729|lt=Yamaguchi Castle|1=Yamaguchi Castle}}. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 20 ({{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}}), Brigadier General Ken R. Dyke, first director of GHQ/CIE, inspected this shrine's festival. ===Shrine buildings / precinct=== *Honden: {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}}, built in 1927. *Shichisha Shrine: moved into the precincts from an area scheduled to be submerged by {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Reservoir|Q11467481|WD=Q11467481|1=Yamaguchi Reservoir}}. ===Festivals=== *Spring grand festival is on the first Sunday of April; autumn grand festival is on Sports Day in October. ===Location / transportation=== *Saitama Prefecture, Tokorozawa, Yamaguchi 1849 **15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shimo-Yamaguchi Station|Q7496941|WD=Q7496941|1=Shimo-Yamaguchi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Sayama Line|Q1965549|WD=Q1965549|1=Seibu Sayama Line}} ===Managed / served shrines=== *Managed shrines **{{ill|lt=Sengen Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sengen Shrine}} (Arahata Fuji) **{{ill|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine}} *Served shrine (outside precinct) **{{ill|lt=Atago Gongen-sha (Tokorozawa)|Q17191381|WD=Q17191381|lt=Atago Gongen-sha|1=Atago Daigongen Shrine}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}|volume=Vol. 158, Iruma District No.3|chapter=Hikawa village, Hikawa shrine|id={{NDLJP|764001/72}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} ===External links=== *[http://nakahikawa.or.jp/ Naka-Hikawa Shrine homepage] ===See also=== *{{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596|1=Hikawa shinkō}} *{{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633|1=Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)}} *{{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942|1=Oku-Hikawa Shrine}} *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334|1=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province}} *{{ill|lt=Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)|Q17226968|WD=Q17226968|1=Nakahikawa Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の氷川神社]] [[Category:所沢市の歴史]] [[Category:所沢市の建築物]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Saitama Prefecture]][[Category:qq]]

Tokorozawa)}} [[JA:中氷川神社 (所沢市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11366320}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|Naka-Hikawa Shrine in Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa|Naka-Hikawa Shrine in Mikajima, Tokorozawa|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)}} {{神社 |名称 = Naka-Hikawa Shrine |画像 = [[File:Naka-Hikawa-jinja (Tokorozawa Yamaguchi) 01.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}Yamaguchi 1849 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}
{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) candidate shrine
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = First Sunday of April, and Sports Day in October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=46|lat_sec=43.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.8|label=Naka-Hikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)}} '''Naka-Hikawa Shrine''' (なかひかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596|1=Hikawa shinkō}} in {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi (Tokorozawa)|Q11466558|WD=Q11466558|lt=Yamaguchi|1=Yamaguchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It was the general guardian of 92 villages in former {{ill|lt=Iruma District|Q1153801|WD=Q1153801|1=Iruma District, Saitama}} and {{ill|lt=Tama District|Q11430789|WD=Q11430789|1=Tama district}}, and was a former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Overview === * It is a candidate shrine for Naka-Hikawa-sha, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Like Ōmiya {{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633|1=Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)}}, it enshrines three deities: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}, and {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}. * "Naka" in Naka-Hikawa is explained as: midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and {{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942|1=Oku-Hikawa Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Okutama|Q598626|WD=Q598626|1=Okutama, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Nishitama District|Q1048689|WD=Q1048689|1=Nishitama District}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}), or midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and the {{ill|lt=provincial capital|Q1629088|WD=Q1629088|1=Kokufu}} of {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}} ({{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}}). * Locally it has long been called "Hikawa-sama." === History === * Traditionally founded in the reign of Emperor Sujin. * In late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Ietsugu|Q11466765|WD=Q11466765|1=Yamaguchi Ietsugu}} built shrine buildings. They later burned in warfare, but in the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era (1573-1593), {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Takatada|Q11467543|WD=Q11467543|1=Yamaguchi Takatada}} restored it together with {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Castle (Musashi)|Q11466729|WD=Q11466729|lt=Yamaguchi Castle|1=Yamaguchi Castle}}. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 20 ({{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}}), Brigadier General Ken R. Dyke, first director of GHQ/CIE, inspected this shrine's festival. ===Shrine buildings / precinct=== *Honden: {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}}, built in 1927. *Shichisha Shrine: moved into the precincts from an area scheduled to be submerged by {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Reservoir|Q11467481|WD=Q11467481|1=Yamaguchi Reservoir}}. ===Festivals=== *Spring grand festival is on the first Sunday of April; autumn grand festival is on Sports Day in October. ===Location / transportation=== *Saitama Prefecture, Tokorozawa, Yamaguchi 1849 **15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shimo-Yamaguchi Station|Q7496941|WD=Q7496941|1=Shimo-Yamaguchi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Sayama Line|Q1965549|WD=Q1965549|1=Seibu Sayama Line}} ===Managed / served shrines=== *Managed shrines **{{ill|lt=Sengen Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sengen Shrine}} (Arahata Fuji) **{{ill|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine}} *Served shrine (outside precinct) **{{ill|lt=Atago Gongen-sha (Tokorozawa)|Q17191381|WD=Q17191381|lt=Atago Gongen-sha|1=Atago Daigongen Shrine}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}|volume=Vol. 158, Iruma District No.3|chapter=Hikawa village, Hikawa shrine|id={{NDLJP|764001/72}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} ===External links=== *[http://nakahikawa.or.jp/ Naka-Hikawa Shrine homepage] ===See also=== *{{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596|1=Hikawa shinkō}} *{{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633|1=Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)}} *{{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942|1=Oku-Hikawa Shrine}} *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334|1=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province}} *{{ill|lt=Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)|Q17226968|WD=Q17226968|1=Nakahikawa Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Hikawa-jinja in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:所沢市の歴史]] [[Category:所沢市の建築物]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Saitama Prefecture]][[Category:qq]]

Tokorozawa) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Nakahikahano Shrine|lt=Nakahikahano Shrine|WD=Q135039739|1=Nakahikahano Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '埼玉県所沢市山口1850', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Naka-hikawa-jinja (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)}} [[JA:中氷川神社 (所沢市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11366320}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|Naka-Hikawa Shrine in Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa|Naka-Hikawa Shrine in Mikajima, Tokorozawa|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)}} {{神社 |名称 = Naka-Hikawa Shrine |画像 = [[File:Naka-Hikawa-jinja (Tokorozawa Yamaguchi) 01.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}Yamaguchi 1849 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}
{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) candidate shrine
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = First Sunday of April, and Sports Day in October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=46|lat_sec=43.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.8|label=Naka-Hikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)}} '''Naka-Hikawa Shrine''' (なかひかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596|1=Hikawa shinkō}} in {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi (Tokorozawa)|Q11466558|WD=Q11466558|lt=Yamaguchi|1=Yamaguchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It was the general guardian of 92 villages in former {{ill|lt=Iruma District|Q1153801|WD=Q1153801|1=Iruma District, Saitama}} and {{ill|lt=Tama District|Q11430789|WD=Q11430789|1=Tama district}}, and was a former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Overview === * It is a candidate shrine for Naka-Hikawa-sha, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Like Ōmiya {{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633|1=Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)}}, it enshrines three deities: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}, and {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}. * "Naka" in Naka-Hikawa is explained as: midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and {{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942|1=Oku-Hikawa Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Okutama|Q598626|WD=Q598626|1=Okutama, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Nishitama District|Q1048689|WD=Q1048689|1=Nishitama District}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}), or midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and the {{ill|lt=provincial capital|Q1629088|WD=Q1629088|1=Kokufu}} of {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}} ({{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}}). * Locally it has long been called "Hikawa-sama." === History === * Traditionally founded in the reign of Emperor Sujin. * In late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Ietsugu|Q11466765|WD=Q11466765|1=Yamaguchi Ietsugu}} built shrine buildings. They later burned in warfare, but in the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era (1573-1593), {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Takatada|Q11467543|WD=Q11467543|1=Yamaguchi Takatada}} restored it together with {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Castle (Musashi)|Q11466729|WD=Q11466729|lt=Yamaguchi Castle|1=Yamaguchi Castle}}. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 20 ({{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}}), Brigadier General Ken R. Dyke, first director of GHQ/CIE, inspected this shrine's festival. ===Shrine buildings / precinct=== *Honden: {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}}, built in 1927. *Shichisha Shrine: moved into the precincts from an area scheduled to be submerged by {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Reservoir|Q11467481|WD=Q11467481|1=Yamaguchi Reservoir}}. ===Festivals=== *Spring grand festival is on the first Sunday of April; autumn grand festival is on Sports Day in October. ===Location / transportation=== *Saitama Prefecture, Tokorozawa, Yamaguchi 1849 **15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shimo-Yamaguchi Station|Q7496941|WD=Q7496941|1=Shimo-Yamaguchi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Sayama Line|Q1965549|WD=Q1965549|1=Seibu Sayama Line}} ===Managed / served shrines=== *Managed shrines **{{ill|lt=Sengen Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sengen Shrine}} (Arahata Fuji) **{{ill|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine}} *Served shrine (outside precinct) **{{ill|lt=Atago Gongen-sha (Tokorozawa)|Q17191381|WD=Q17191381|lt=Atago Gongen-sha|1=Atago Daigongen Shrine}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}|volume=Vol. 158, Iruma District No.3|chapter=Hikawa village, Hikawa shrine|id={{NDLJP|764001/72}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} ===External links=== *[http://nakahikawa.or.jp/ Naka-Hikawa Shrine homepage] ===See also=== *{{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596|1=Hikawa shinkō}} *{{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633|1=Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)}} *{{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942|1=Oku-Hikawa Shrine}} *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334|1=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province}} *{{ill|lt=Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)|Q17226968|WD=Q17226968|1=Nakahikawa Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '埼玉県所沢市山口1850', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Naka-hikawa-jinja (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)}} [[JA:中氷川神社 (所沢市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11366320}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|Naka-Hikawa Shrine in Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa|Naka-Hikawa Shrine in Mikajima, Tokorozawa|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)}} {{神社 |名称 = Naka-Hikawa Shrine |画像 = [[File:Naka-Hikawa-jinja (Tokorozawa Yamaguchi) 01.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}Yamaguchi 1849 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}
{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) candidate shrine
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = First Sunday of April, and Sports Day in October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=46|lat_sec=43.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.8|label=Naka-Hikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)}} '''Naka-Hikawa Shrine''' (なかひかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596|1=Hikawa shinkō}} in {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi (Tokorozawa)|Q11466558|WD=Q11466558|lt=Yamaguchi|1=Yamaguchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It was the general guardian of 92 villages in former {{ill|lt=Iruma District|Q1153801|WD=Q1153801|1=Iruma District, Saitama}} and {{ill|lt=Tama District|Q11430789|WD=Q11430789|1=Tama district}}, and was a former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Overview === * It is a candidate shrine for Naka-Hikawa-sha, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Like Ōmiya {{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633|1=Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)}}, it enshrines three deities: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}, and {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}. * "Naka" in Naka-Hikawa is explained as: midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and {{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942|1=Oku-Hikawa Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Okutama|Q598626|WD=Q598626|1=Okutama, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Nishitama District|Q1048689|WD=Q1048689|1=Nishitama District}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}), or midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and the {{ill|lt=provincial capital|Q1629088|WD=Q1629088|1=Kokufu}} of {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}} ({{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}}). * Locally it has long been called "Hikawa-sama." === History === * Traditionally founded in the reign of Emperor Sujin. * In late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Ietsugu|Q11466765|WD=Q11466765|1=Yamaguchi Ietsugu}} built shrine buildings. They later burned in warfare, but in the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era (1573-1593), {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Takatada|Q11467543|WD=Q11467543|1=Yamaguchi Takatada}} restored it together with {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Castle (Musashi)|Q11466729|WD=Q11466729|lt=Yamaguchi Castle|1=Yamaguchi Castle}}. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 20 ({{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}}), Brigadier General Ken R. Dyke, first director of GHQ/CIE, inspected this shrine's festival. ===Shrine buildings / precinct=== *Honden: {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}}, built in 1927. *Shichisha Shrine: moved into the precincts from an area scheduled to be submerged by {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Reservoir|Q11467481|WD=Q11467481|1=Yamaguchi Reservoir}}. ===Festivals=== *Spring grand festival is on the first Sunday of April; autumn grand festival is on Sports Day in October. ===Location / transportation=== *Saitama Prefecture, Tokorozawa, Yamaguchi 1849 **15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shimo-Yamaguchi Station|Q7496941|WD=Q7496941|1=Shimo-Yamaguchi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Sayama Line|Q1965549|WD=Q1965549|1=Seibu Sayama Line}} ===Managed / served shrines=== *Managed shrines **{{ill|lt=Sengen Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sengen Shrine}} (Arahata Fuji) **{{ill|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine}} *Served shrine (outside precinct) **{{ill|lt=Atago Gongen-sha (Tokorozawa)|Q17191381|WD=Q17191381|lt=Atago Gongen-sha|1=Atago Daigongen Shrine}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}|volume=Vol. 158, Iruma District No.3|chapter=Hikawa village, Hikawa shrine|id={{NDLJP|764001/72}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} ===External links=== *[http://nakahikawa.or.jp/ Naka-Hikawa Shrine homepage] ===See also=== *{{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596|1=Hikawa shinkō}} *{{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633|1=Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)}} *{{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942|1=Oku-Hikawa Shrine}} *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334|1=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province}} *{{ill|lt=Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)|Q17226968|WD=Q17226968|1=Nakahikawa Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}Yamaguchi 1849 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}

{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) candidate shrine
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = First Sunday of April, and Sports Day in October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=46|lat_sec=43.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.8|label=Naka-Hikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)}} '''Naka-Hikawa Shrine''' (なかひかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596|1=Hikawa shinkō}} in {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi (Tokorozawa)|Q11466558|WD=Q11466558|lt=Yamaguchi|1=Yamaguchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It was the general guardian of 92 villages in former {{ill|lt=Iruma District|Q1153801|WD=Q1153801|1=Iruma District, Saitama}} and {{ill|lt=Tama District|Q11430789|WD=Q11430789|1=Tama district}}, and was a former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Overview === * It is a candidate shrine for Naka-Hikawa-sha, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Like Ōmiya {{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633|1=Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)}}, it enshrines three deities: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}, and {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}. * "Naka" in Naka-Hikawa is explained as: midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and {{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942|1=Oku-Hikawa Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Okutama|Q598626|WD=Q598626|1=Okutama, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Nishitama District|Q1048689|WD=Q1048689|1=Nishitama District}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}), or midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and the {{ill|lt=provincial capital|Q1629088|WD=Q1629088|1=Kokufu}} of {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}} ({{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}}). * Locally it has long been called "Hikawa-sama." === History === * Traditionally founded in the reign of Emperor Sujin. * In late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Ietsugu|Q11466765|WD=Q11466765|1=Yamaguchi Ietsugu}} built shrine buildings. They later burned in warfare, but in the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era (1573-1593), {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Takatada|Q11467543|WD=Q11467543|1=Yamaguchi Takatada}} restored it together with {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Castle (Musashi)|Q11466729|WD=Q11466729|lt=Yamaguchi Castle|1=Yamaguchi Castle}}. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 20 ({{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}}), Brigadier General Ken R. Dyke, first director of GHQ/CIE, inspected this shrine's festival. ===Shrine buildings / precinct=== *Honden: {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}}, built in 1927. *Shichisha Shrine: moved into the precincts from an area scheduled to be submerged by {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Reservoir|Q11467481|WD=Q11467481|1=Yamaguchi Reservoir}}. ===Festivals=== *Spring grand festival is on the first Sunday of April; autumn grand festival is on Sports Day in October. ===Location / transportation=== *Saitama Prefecture, Tokorozawa, Yamaguchi 1849 **15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shimo-Yamaguchi Station|Q7496941|WD=Q7496941|1=Shimo-Yamaguchi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Sayama Line|Q1965549|WD=Q1965549|1=Seibu Sayama Line}} ===Managed / served shrines=== *Managed shrines **{{ill|lt=Sengen Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sengen Shrine}} (Arahata Fuji) **{{ill|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine}} *Served shrine (outside precinct) Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}Yamaguchi 1849 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}

{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) candidate shrine
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = First Sunday of April, and Sports Day in October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=46|lat_sec=43.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.8|label=Naka-Hikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)}} '''Naka-Hikawa Shrine''' (なかひかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596|1=Hikawa shinkō}} in {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi (Tokorozawa)|Q11466558|WD=Q11466558|lt=Yamaguchi|1=Yamaguchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It was the general guardian of 92 villages in former {{ill|lt=Iruma District|Q1153801|WD=Q1153801|1=Iruma District, Saitama}} and {{ill|lt=Tama District|Q11430789|WD=Q11430789|1=Tama district}}, and was a former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Overview === * It is a candidate shrine for Naka-Hikawa-sha, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Like Ōmiya {{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633|1=Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)}}, it enshrines three deities: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}, and {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}. * "Naka" in Naka-Hikawa is explained as: midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and {{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942|1=Oku-Hikawa Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Okutama|Q598626|WD=Q598626|1=Okutama, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Nishitama District|Q1048689|WD=Q1048689|1=Nishitama District}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}), or midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and the {{ill|lt=provincial capital|Q1629088|WD=Q1629088|1=Kokufu}} of {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}} ({{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}}). * Locally it has long been called "Hikawa-sama." === History === * Traditionally founded in the reign of Emperor Sujin. * In late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Ietsugu|Q11466765|WD=Q11466765|1=Yamaguchi Ietsugu}} built shrine buildings. They later burned in warfare, but in the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era (1573-1593), {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Takatada|Q11467543|WD=Q11467543|1=Yamaguchi Takatada}} restored it together with {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Castle (Musashi)|Q11466729|WD=Q11466729|lt=Yamaguchi Castle|1=Yamaguchi Castle}}. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 20 ({{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}}), Brigadier General Ken R. Dyke, first director of GHQ/CIE, inspected this shrine's festival. ===Shrine buildings / precinct=== *Honden: {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}}, built in 1927. *Shichisha Shrine: moved into the precincts from an area scheduled to be submerged by {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Reservoir|Q11467481|WD=Q11467481|1=Yamaguchi Reservoir}}. ===Festivals=== *Spring grand festival is on the first Sunday of April; autumn grand festival is on Sports Day in October. ===Location / transportation=== *Saitama Prefecture, Tokorozawa, Yamaguchi 1849 **15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shimo-Yamaguchi Station|Q7496941|WD=Q7496941|1=Shimo-Yamaguchi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Sayama Line|Q1965549|WD=Q1965549|1=Seibu Sayama Line}} ===Managed / served shrines=== *Managed shrines **{{ill|lt=Sengen Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sengen Shrine}} (Arahata Fuji) **{{ill|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine}} *Served shrine (outside precinct) Tokorozawa)}} [[JA:中氷川神社 (所沢市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11366320}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|Naka-Hikawa Shrine in Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa|Naka-Hikawa Shrine in Mikajima, Tokorozawa|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)}} {{神社 |名称 = Naka-Hikawa Shrine |画像 = [[File:Naka-Hikawa-jinja (Tokorozawa Yamaguchi) 01.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}Yamaguchi 1849 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}

{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) candidate shrine
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = First Sunday of April, and Sports Day in October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=46|lat_sec=43.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=25|lon_sec=48.8|label=Naka-Hikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|46|43.8|N|139|25|48.8|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)|Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa)}} '''Naka-Hikawa Shrine''' (なかひかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596|1=Hikawa shinkō}} in {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi (Tokorozawa)|Q11466558|WD=Q11466558|lt=Yamaguchi|1=Yamaguchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It was the general guardian of 92 villages in former {{ill|lt=Iruma District|Q1153801|WD=Q1153801|1=Iruma District, Saitama}} and {{ill|lt=Tama District|Q11430789|WD=Q11430789|1=Tama district}}, and was a former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Overview === * It is a candidate shrine for Naka-Hikawa-sha, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Like Ōmiya {{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633|1=Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)}}, it enshrines three deities: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}, and {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}. * "Naka" in Naka-Hikawa is explained as: midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and {{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942|1=Oku-Hikawa Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Okutama|Q598626|WD=Q598626|1=Okutama, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Nishitama District|Q1048689|WD=Q1048689|1=Nishitama District}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}), or midway between Ōmiya Hikawa Shrine and the {{ill|lt=provincial capital|Q1629088|WD=Q1629088|1=Kokufu}} of {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}} ({{ill|lt=Fuchū, Tokyo|Q208818|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}}). * Locally it has long been called "Hikawa-sama." === History === * Traditionally founded in the reign of Emperor Sujin. * In late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Ietsugu|Q11466765|WD=Q11466765|1=Yamaguchi Ietsugu}} built shrine buildings. They later burned in warfare, but in the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era (1573-1593), {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Takatada|Q11467543|WD=Q11467543|1=Yamaguchi Takatada}} restored it together with {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Castle (Musashi)|Q11466729|WD=Q11466729|lt=Yamaguchi Castle|1=Yamaguchi Castle}}. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 20 ({{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}}), Brigadier General Ken R. Dyke, first director of GHQ/CIE, inspected this shrine's festival. ===Shrine buildings / precinct=== *Honden: {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}}, built in 1927. *Shichisha Shrine: moved into the precincts from an area scheduled to be submerged by {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Reservoir|Q11467481|WD=Q11467481|1=Yamaguchi Reservoir}}. ===Festivals=== *Spring grand festival is on the first Sunday of April; autumn grand festival is on Sports Day in October. ===Location / transportation=== *Saitama Prefecture, Tokorozawa, Yamaguchi 1849 **15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shimo-Yamaguchi Station|Q7496941|WD=Q7496941|1=Shimo-Yamaguchi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Sayama Line|Q1965549|WD=Q1965549|1=Seibu Sayama Line}} ===Managed / served shrines=== *Managed shrines **{{ill|lt=Sengen Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sengen Shrine}} (Arahata Fuji) **{{ill|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenma Tenjin Shrine}} *Served shrine (outside precinct) **{{ill|lt=Atago Gongen-sha (Tokorozawa)|Q17191381|WD=Q17191381|lt=Atago Gongen-sha|1=Atago Daigongen Shrine}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}|volume=Vol. 158, Iruma District No.3|chapter=Hikawa village, Hikawa shrine|id={{NDLJP|764001/72}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} ===External links=== *[http://nakahikawa.or.jp/ Naka-Hikawa Shrine homepage] ===See also=== *{{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596|1=Hikawa shinkō}} *{{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633|1=Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)}} *{{ill|lt=Oku-Hikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942|1=Oku-Hikawa Shrine}} *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334|1=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province}} *{{ill|lt=Naka-Hikawa Shrine (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)|Q17226968|WD=Q17226968|1=Nakahikawa Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka-Hikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Hikawa-jinja in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tokorozawa]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tokorozawa, Saitama]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Saitama Prefecture]]
